articles.title: NASA Awards Solutions for Federal Enterprise Procurement Contracts
articles.summary: NASA will begin processing the awards of multiple contracts for the Solutions for Enterprise‑wide Procurement (SEWP) VI Government-wide Acquisition Contract. The contract provides streamlined access to commercial products and services, including hardware, software, cloud services, cybersecurity tools, engineering and consulting services, and data intensive mission support capabilities. This competitive acquisition was conducted within three categories: […]

articles.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
articles.summary: OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month. Calling GPT‑5.5‑Cyber its "strongest model yet for finding and helping patch software vulnerabilities," OpenAI said the model can "sustain deeper analysis across large codebases" to

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
articles.summary: Deadly drone and missile attacks in Ukraine over the weekend represented a “dangerous cycle of escalation” in Russia’s full-scale four-year-old war, the Security Council heard on Monday from Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General in the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs and Peace Operations, while Edem Wosornu, crisis response director at the UN relief office, OCHA, warned that “the choices made here can mean the difference between lives saved or lives lost.”

articles.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to stealthily read artificial intelligence (AI) conversions from other customers' applications without requiring authentication. The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed DifyTap by Zafran Security.

articles.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
articles.summary: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

articles.title: NASA’s Experimental Fabrication Branch Fuels Aircraft Innovation
articles.summary: At NASA, innovation begins well before an aircraft takes flight. The Experimental Fabrication Branch at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, transforms engineering concepts into mission‑ready hardware for research aircraft and technology development. This capability helps the agency deliver advancements that benefit the public by improving aviation safety, efficiency, and sustainability. The branch […]

articles.title: Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services
articles.summary: The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund.

articles.title: Climate crisis: UN chief lays out solutions blueprint for clean energy transition
articles.summary: As a deadly heatwave continued to grip Europe on Tuesday, UN Secretary-General António Guterres issued an impassioned appeal for more ambitious global action on climate change caused by fossil fuels, to prevent irreversible damage.

articles.title: NASA’s Webb Finds Clues to Ancient, Distant Origin of Comet 3I/ATLAS
articles.summary: As interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS began moving away from the Sun in December 2025, astronomers took the opportunity to turn NASA’s powerful James Webb Space Telescope in its direction and capture detailed measurements of its chemical components. The comet was freshly warmed from its closest pass by the Sun, and its ancient ice had been converted […]

articles.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. According to Elastic Security Labs, the campaign leverages malicious Google Ads as a starting point to distribute the malware. articles.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
articles.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

articles.title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
articles.summary: Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remain in dispute 12 months after he died.

articles.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
articles.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

articles.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
articles.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

articles.title: AI is getting women wrong as gender bias persists, data reveals
articles.summary: As artificial intelligence reshapes how people work, communicate and access information, UN Women warned on Monday that the technology is reproducing old gender stereotypes, which amplify online abuse and leave women out of the decisions that will define the digital future.

articles.title: NASA’s Chandra Finds Possible Supernova Remnant
articles.summary: Using data from NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory, astronomers may have found a supernova remnant – seen in this June 11, 2026, image – in an intriguing neighborhood in the middle of the Milky Way galaxy. Supernova remnants are the expanding remains of exploded stars and provide elements like iron, oxygen, and silicon that are critical […]

articles.summary: NASA astronauts will venture outside the International Space Station on Tuesday, June 30, to replace a wrist joint on the orbital complex’s Canadarm2 robotic arm. articles.summary: India is home to about 60% of the world’s wild Asian elephants, and around 80% of the animals’ habitat lies outside protected areas, according to the Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change. articles.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT). articles.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
articles.summary: Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software. articles.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code. articles.title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
articles.summary: Canada's spy service got a judge's permission to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear sitting on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets. The Federal Court released a public version of the ruling on June 15. It is the first time the Canadian Security Intelligence Service has used its threat reduction warrant powers this way. articles.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
articles.summary: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

articles.title: Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia
articles.summary: Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented.

articles.title: Australia's coal and gas exports violate our human rights, group says in new UN case
articles.summary: The group says that it is unlawful for Australia to continue approving fossil fuel exports without protecting its citizens.

articles.title: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
articles.summary: Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.

articles.title: Sudan: Drone attacks endanger civilians and shut down critical services in El Obeid
articles.summary: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.

articles.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears
articles.summary: Syria has emerged after more than a decade of crisis with “clear signs of progress”, but acute needs remain and efforts must boost support so the country can “move from survival towards recovery”, UN officials told the Security Council on Monday.

articles.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
articles.summary: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance between the warrior and the wound grew wider, and yet one thing never moved: a human chose the target

articles.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
articles.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

articles.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
articles.summary: The note from the possible kidnappers reportedly stated that they did not mean for her to die and included an apology to the family.

articles.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
articles.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

articles.title: Seabed damaged by fishing showing signs of recovery
articles.summary: Campaigners say improvements to the area trashed by illegal dredging show that marine protection works.

articles.title: Powering resilience: How Indonesia is building a more secure energy future
articles.summary: As countries grapple with energy price shocks, supply disruptions and the growing impacts of climate change, energy security has become a pressing concern.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Pope Leo urges action on hunger, humanitarian strain deepens in Gaza, families return to Lebanon
articles.summary: Pope Leo called on the international community to renew its commitment to tackling hunger and malnutrition, describing access to adequate food as a “fundamental human right” during a visit to the World Food Programme (WFP) headquarters in Rome on Monday.

articles.title: World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk
articles.summary: Four decades after AIDS first emerged as a global crisis, world leaders, advocates and community representatives gathered at UN Headquarters on Monday issued a stark warning: progress against HIV is slowing just as financial pressures and shrinking support threaten to reverse decades of gains.
